message:
fix(websocket): close handshake resilience and continuation frame rejection

client.rs, server.rs, split.rs:
  On receiving a Close frame, mark_response_sent() is now called
  BEFORE propagating any send_frame error. Previously, if the close
  response frame failed to send (e.g., broken pipe), the handshake
  state was never updated, leaving is_closed() returning false and
  potentially causing the connection to hang in a half-closed limbo.
  Now the close handshake transitions to closed regardless of whether
  the response was successfully written.

client.rs:
  Reject bare Continuation frames in Message::try_from(Frame) with
  a ProtocolViolation error instead of silently treating them as
  Binary. Continuation frames only make sense within a multi-frame
  message sequence managed by a message assembler; accepting them
  in isolation would produce corrupt messages.

TestIo (all three files):
  Expand TestIo mock to support configurable read data, read position
  tracking, and synthetic write failures (fail_writes flag). Add
  encode_server_frame/encode_client_frame helpers for constructing
  valid wire-format frames for test scenarios.

Tests:
  Add recv_marks_close_response_sent_even_if_send_fails regression
  test in all three modules (client, server, split) verifying that
  a broken pipe during close response still transitions to closed.
  Add continuation frame rejection test in client.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
